response: Charles A. Agemian and Arshile Gorky both experienced significant career changes, but the nature and extent of these changes differ. Charles A. Agemian retired from Chase Manhattan Bank and became chairman and chief executive officer of the Hackensack Trust Co., later renamed Garden State National Bank. He had a career in banking before transitioning to the arts. Arshile Gorky, on the other hand, was an artist whose career was deeply influenced by his experiences, including the Armenian Genocide, and he transitioned from a national of the United States to a prominent figure in Abstract Expressionism. Both individuals had notable shifts in their professional paths, but Gorky's career changes were more closely tied to his artistic development and personal history.
